帮.origin: Think of a nation's 邦 raising its cloth 巾 flag — neighbors come together to help. 邦 also hints at the sound.

sentence.en: Let me help you carry your school bag.

助.origin: Add your strength 力 to someone else — that extra muscle on the right means to help.

sentence.en: Thank you for helping me.

林.origin: Two trees 木 standing side by side make a little woods — many of them become a forest.

sentence.en: The little bird lives in the woods.

怕.origin: A heart 忄 that turns white 白 with fright — that shaky feeling is being afraid.

sentence.en: I am not afraid of the dark.

妻.origin: A woman 女 holding a broom in her hand 彐, busy at home — she is the wife.

sentence.en: His wife is very kind.

尖.origin: Small 小 sitting on top of big 大 — narrowing to a tiny point at the tip makes it sharp.

sentence.en: The tip of the pencil is very sharp.

志.origin: A scholar 士 on top of a heart 心 — what your heart sets out to do is your will. 士 also gives the sound.

sentence.en: He has had big dreams since he was little.

克.origin: Picture a strong child 兄 carrying a heavy load 十 on top yet pushing through — to overcome.

sentence.en: He overcame the difficulty.

困.origin: A tree 木 boxed inside a fence 囗 — trapped and stuck, just like being sleepy and tired.

sentence.en: I am very sleepy today.

取.origin: An ear 耳 next to a hand 又 — reach out with your hand and take something.

sentence.en: I am going to fetch my book.

胜.origin: On the left is flesh ⺼; the right 生 gives the sound. Together it means to win and feel alive.

sentence.en: Our team won the victory.

守.origin: A hand 寸 kept safe under a roof 宀 — staying inside to guard and protect the home.

sentence.en: The big dog guards the front door.

景.origin: The sun 日 shining over a tall city 京 lights up the lovely scenery. 京 also gives the sound.

sentence.en: The scenery here is really beautiful.

争.origin: Two hands reaching from above and below for the same thing 亅 — pulling and tugging is to fight over it.

sentence.en: They are fighting over a toy.

管.origin: Bamboo ⺮ on top of an official 官 — a hollow bamboo tube, and also the one in charge who manages.

sentence.en: Mom is in charge of my homework.

凹.origin: Look at its shape — it dips down in the middle like a little bowl. That sunken dent means concave.

sentence.en: The middle of this ground is sunken.

凸.origin: Look at its shape — a bump pokes up in the middle. That sticking-out part means to bulge out.

sentence.en: His forehead sticks out a little.

楚.origin: Two trees 林 over feet 疋 — picture walking through clear, open woods where everything looks distinct.

sentence.en: I can see very clearly.

且.origin: A picture of a stacked stone altar — its steady layers help you remember 'moreover, and also.'

sentence.en: He is clever, and also hard-working.

倍.origin: A person 亻 beside 咅 — imagine standing next to your twin, so now there are two: double.

sentence.en: Today there are twice as many people.

似.origin: One person 亻 standing beside 以 another — two people looking so alike means similar.

sentence.en: The two of them look very similar.

乎.origin: A little curling shape like a puff of breath rising — it ends questions, like 'oh?' in old stories.

sentence.en: I almost forgot.

继.origin: Silk thread 纟 on the left — when one thread joins the next, the line continues on.

sentence.en: Let's keep playing.

续.origin: Silk thread 纟 on the left keeps spinning out without stopping — going on and on, serial.

sentence.en: He read three books in a row.

研.origin: A stone 石 you rub flat 开 — grinding it down little by little, just like studying a problem closely.

sentence.en: He loves studying insects.

究.origin: Dig deep into a cave 穴 right to the very end 九 — to investigate and find out everything.

sentence.en: Scientists are studying the moon.

辞.origin: A tongue 舌 next to the sharp word 辛 — careful spoken words, and saying goodbye.

word.en: to say goodbye, to take leave

sentence.en: He said goodbye to everyone before leaving.

陵.origin: A mound shape 阝 on the left where people climb 夌 — a tall hill. 夌 also gives the sound.

sentence.en: The hill is covered with trees.

猿.origin: The animal radical 犭 on the left tells you it's a creature; 袁 gives the sound — a long-armed ape.

sentence.en: There is an ape in the tree.

舟.origin: A picture of a little boat with its curved sides riding the water — it means boat.

sentence.en: A little boat is on the river.

庐.origin: A roof 广 over a door 户 — a tiny home tucked under the eaves, a simple hut. 户 also gives the sound.

sentence.en: There is a little hut in the mountains.

瀑.origin: Water 氵 crashing down violently 暴 — that roaring rush off a cliff is a waterfall. 暴 also gives the sound.

sentence.en: The waterfall flows down from the mountain.

炉.origin: A fire 火 burning by the door 户 of your home — a warm stove or furnace.

sentence.en: It is very warm beside the stove.

紫.origin: Silk 糸 dyed below the top part 此 — mixing red and blue threads makes the color purple.

sentence.en: I like purple flowers.

烟.origin: Fire 火 on the left; 因 gives the sound. Where there's fire, gray smoke curls up.

sentence.en: Smoke comes out of the chimney.

遥.origin: The walking radical 辶 means a journey; here the path stretches far and far away — remote.

sentence.en: He comes from a faraway place.

川.origin: Three flowing lines like water running between banks — picture a river streaming along.

sentence.en: The little river flows through the mountains and streams.

尺.origin: Picture a person bending down to measure by pacing — the tool for measuring is a ruler.

sentence.en: I draw lines with a ruler.

篇.origin: Bamboo ⺮ on top, flat 扁 below — old books were flat bamboo strips, so this counts a chapter. 扁 gives the sound.

sentence.en: I wrote an essay.

颂.origin: A head or page 页 on the right tells the family; 公 gives the sound — to praise out loud.

sentence.en: This song praises the hero.
